description: All tasks required to deliver a fully functioning backend with complete test coverage. Tasks are ordered by dependency — complete them in sequence unless marked as parallelizable.

tasks.name: Project Scaffold & Configuration
tasks.description: Initialize the backend Node.js project with all required dependencies and configuration files. This task is complete when: (1) `backend/package.json` exists with all runtime dependencies (express, ws, prisma, @prisma/client, bcrypt, jsonwebtoken, dotenv, cors) and all dev dependencies (typescript, ts-node-dev, @types/* packages). (2) `backend/tsconfig.json` exists with strict mode enabled, target ES2020, module commonjs, outDir dist, rootDir src. (3) `backend/.env.example` exists listing all required env vars (DATABASE_URL, JWT_ACCESS_SECRET, JWT_REFRESH_SECRET, JWT_ACCESS_EXPIRES_IN, JWT_REFRESH_EXPIRES_IN, PORT, CLIENT_ORIGIN) with placeholder values. (4) `backend/.gitignore` ignores node_modules, dist, .env, and the SQLite database file. (5) `npm run dev` starts the server without TypeScript errors. (6) `npm run build` compiles successfully to the dist/ folder.

tasks.name: Prisma Schema & Initial Migration
tasks.description: Define the complete Prisma schema and run the initial migration to create the SQLite database. This task is complete when: (1) `backend/prisma/schema.prisma` defines all six models exactly as specified in docs/schema.md: User, RefreshToken, Folder, Note, NoteShare, FolderShare. (2) All relations are correctly defined (User→Notes, User→Folders, User→RefreshTokens, User→NoteShares, User→FolderShares, Folder self-relation for nesting, Folder→Notes, Note→NoteShares, Folder→FolderShares). (3) All indexes listed in docs/schema.md are present. (4) `onDelete` cascade rules match the schema doc: User deletion cascades to all owned resources; Note deletion cascades NoteShares; Folder deletion sets Note.folderId to null. (5) `npx prisma migrate dev` runs without errors and creates the SQLite DB file. (6) `npx prisma generate` runs without errors and the Prisma client is usable in TypeScript. (7) The Prisma client singleton is created at `backend/src/lib/prisma.ts` and exported as a named export `prisma`.

tasks.name: Express App Bootstrap & Middleware Setup
tasks.description: Create the Express application entry point with all global middleware registered in the correct order. This task is complete when: (1) `backend/src/index.ts` creates an Express app and an HTTP server (using `http.createServer(app)`). (2) `express.json()` middleware is registered. (3) CORS middleware is registered using the `CLIENT_ORIGIN` env var to restrict allowed origins. (4) The app listens on the `PORT` env var, defaulting to 3000. (5) A `GET /api/health` endpoint returns `{ status: 'ok' }` with status 200 — useful for smoke testing. (6) The global error handling middleware (from `src/middleware/error.ts`) is registered last, after all routes. (7) `src/middleware/error.ts` catches any thrown error: if it is an `AppError` instance, responds with `{ error: message, code: code }` and the correct HTTP status; if it is an unknown error, responds with `{ error: 'Internal server error', code: 'INTERNAL_ERROR' }` and status 500. (8) `src/types/index.ts` exports the `AppError` class, `AuthUser` interface, `Permission` type, and the Express `Request` augmentation (adding `user?: AuthUser`). (9) `src/middleware/validate.ts` exports the `requireFields` helper as described in docs/conventions.md. (10) Server startup logs the port to console.

tasks.name: Auth Service & Routes
tasks.description: Implement the complete authentication system: register, login, token refresh, and logout. This task is complete when: (1) `src/services/auth.service.ts` contains four exported async functions: `registerUser`, `loginUser`, `refreshTokens`, `logoutUser`. No req/res objects are used inside any service function. (2) `registerUser(email, password)`: validates email format, validates password minimum 8 chars, throws AppError 409 CONFLICT if email already exists, hashes password with bcrypt cost 12, creates User in DB, signs access token (payload: { sub: userId, email }, expires JWT_ACCESS_EXPIRES_IN), signs refresh token (payload: { sub: userId }, expires JWT_REFRESH_EXPIRES_IN), stores refresh token in RefreshToken table with correct expiresAt, returns { user: { id, email }, accessToken, refreshToken }. (3) `loginUser(email, password)`: finds user by email, returns 401 UNAUTHORIZED (same message whether email not found or password wrong — no user enumeration), compares bcrypt hash, on success issues and stores tokens same as register, returns same shape. (4) `refreshTokens(refreshToken)`: verifies JWT signature with JWT_REFRESH_SECRET, throws 401 if invalid/expired, looks up token in DB throws 401 if not found, deletes old token, issues new access + refresh token pair, stores new refresh token, returns { accessToken, refreshToken }. (5) `logoutUser(refreshToken)`: deletes the RefreshToken row from DB (no error if not found — idempotent). (6) `src/controllers/auth.controller.ts` has four handler functions that parse req.body, call the service, and send the correct HTTP response codes (201 for register, 200 for login/refresh, 204 for logout). (7) `src/routes/auth.ts` mounts handlers at POST /register, POST /login, POST /refresh, POST /logout with `requireFields` validation middleware. (8) Auth routes are mounted at `/api/auth` in `src/index.ts`. (9) Password is never included in any response object.

tasks.name: Auth Middleware (JWT Guard)
tasks.description: Implement the JWT authentication middleware used to protect all non-auth routes. This task is complete when: (1) `src/middleware/auth.ts` exports a default Express middleware function. (2) It reads the Authorization header, extracts the Bearer token. (3) It verifies the token using `JWT_ACCESS_SECRET`. (4) On success, it attaches `{ id: payload.sub, email: payload.email }` to `req.user` and calls `next()`. (5) If the header is missing, malformed, or the token is invalid/expired, it throws `AppError(401, 'UNAUTHORIZED', 'Invalid or missing token')`. (6) The middleware does NOT hit the database — access tokens are validated by signature only. (7) The middleware is exported and applied to all routes that require authentication (folders, notes, users).

tasks.name: Users Routes
tasks.description: Implement the two user-facing endpoints. This task is complete when: (1) `src/services/users.service.ts` exports `getMe(userId)` which returns `{ id, email, createdAt }` for the given user (throws 404 if not found, though this should not happen in practice), and `searchUserByEmail(email)` which returns `{ id, email }` for exact email match only (throws 404 NOT_FOUND if no match). (2) `src/controllers/users.controller.ts` has handlers for both endpoints. (3) `src/routes/users.ts` mounts GET /me and GET /search on the auth-protected router. (4) Routes are mounted at `/api/users` in index.ts. (5) `GET /api/users/me` returns the authenticated user's profile without the password field. (6) `GET /api/users/search?email=x` only does exact match — no partial/fuzzy search, no wildcard. Returns 404 if no user found.

tasks.name: Permission Resolution Utility
tasks.description: Implement the shared permission resolution logic used by both notes and folders services. This task is complete when: (1) A utility function `resolveNotePermission(noteId, userId, prisma)` is implemented (in `src/services/permissions.ts` or inline in the notes service) that returns 'owner' | 'write' | 'read' | null following the resolution order from docs/schema.md: owner check first, then direct NoteShare, then FolderShare on the note's folder. (2) A utility function `resolveFolderPermission(folderId, userId, prisma)` returns 'owner' | 'write' | 'read' | null: owner check, then FolderShare row. (3) Both functions return `null` if the user has no access whatsoever. (4) Both functions are used consistently throughout the notes and folders services — the same logic is not duplicated. (5) The resolution order is strictly: owner > direct share > folder share (for notes). Direct note shares override folder-level permissions.

tasks.name: Folders CRUD Routes
tasks.description: Implement all folder CRUD endpoints. This task is complete when: (1) `src/services/folders.service.ts` exports: `listFolders(userId)` returns all folders the user owns or has a FolderShare for, each decorated with a `permission` field ('owner'|'read'|'write'); `createFolder(userId, name, parentId?)` validates the parent exists and user has write access to it (or owns it) if parentId is provided, creates the folder, returns the created folder; `updateFolder(userId, folderId, data)` checks user is owner or has write access, updates name/parentId, returns updated folder; `deleteFolder(userId, folderId)` checks user is owner only (not just write access — only owner can delete), recursively deletes all child folders and notes using Prisma transactions, returns void. (2) `src/controllers/folders.controller.ts` handles all four operations. (3) `src/routes/folders.ts` defines: GET /, POST /, PATCH /:id, DELETE /:id — all protected by auth middleware. (4) Routes mounted at `/api/folders` in index.ts. (5) All error cases return the correct HTTP status and error code as specified in docs/api.md. (6) The `password` field is never included in any response. (7) `listFolders` does not include folder contents (notes/children) in the response — just the folder metadata.

tasks.name: Folder Sharing Routes
tasks.description: Implement the folder sharing sub-routes. This task is complete when: (1) `src/services/folders.service.ts` (or a dedicated sharing service) exports: `listFolderShares(userId, folderId)` — only folder owner can list shares, returns array of { userId, email, permission }; `shareFolderWithUser(ownerId, folderId, targetEmail, permission)` — validates folderId exists and ownerId is the owner, looks up target user by exact email (404 if not found), throws 409 CONFLICT if share already exists, creates FolderShare, returns { userId, email, permission }; `updateFolderShare(ownerId, folderId, targetUserId, permission)` — validates ownership, updates permission, returns updated share; `removeFolderShare(ownerId, folderId, targetUserId)` — validates ownership, deletes the share row, returns void. (2) `src/routes/folders.ts` adds sub-routes: GET /:id/shares, POST /:id/shares, PATCH /:id/shares/:userId, DELETE /:id/shares/:userId. (3) All permission checks use the owner check only — write access is not sufficient to manage shares. (4) Correct HTTP status codes: 200 for list/update, 201 for create, 204 for delete. (5) Sharing a folder with yourself (owner) is rejected with a meaningful error.

tasks.name: Notes CRUD Routes
tasks.description: Implement all note CRUD endpoints. This task is complete when: (1) `src/services/notes.service.ts` exports: `listNotes(userId, folderId?)` returns all notes the user owns or has access to (via NoteShare or FolderShare), optionally filtered by folderId, each decorated with `permission` field — content field is EXCLUDED from list response; `createNote(userId, title, content, folderId?)` validates folder exists and user has write access if folderId provided, creates note, returns full note object including content; `getNote(userId, noteId)` resolves permission for the note, returns full note including content and permission field, throws 403 if no access, 404 if not found; `updateNote(userId, noteId, data)` resolves permission, requires at least 'write' access, updates title/content/folderId (whichever are provided), returns updated full note; `deleteNote(userId, noteId)` requires ownership (not just write), deletes note, returns void. (2) `src/controllers/notes.controller.ts` handles all five operations. (3) `src/routes/notes.ts` defines: GET /, POST /, GET /:id, PATCH /:id, DELETE /:id — all auth-protected. (4) Routes mounted at `/api/notes`. (5) GET /api/notes does NOT return `content` field in any list item. (6) All error codes match docs/api.md exactly.

tasks.name: Note Sharing Routes
tasks.description: Implement the note sharing sub-routes. This task is complete when: (1) `src/services/notes.service.ts` (or a dedicated sharing service) exports: `listNoteShares(userId, noteId)` — only note owner can list, returns array of { userId, email, permission }; `shareNoteWithUser(ownerId, noteId, targetEmail, permission)` — validates ownership, looks up target by email (404 if not found), throws 409 if already shared, creates NoteShare, returns { userId, email, permission }; `updateNoteShare(ownerId, noteId, targetUserId, permission)` — validates ownership, updates permission field, returns updated share object; `removeNoteShare(ownerId, noteId, targetUserId)` — validates ownership, deletes share row. (2) `src/routes/notes.ts` adds: GET /:id/shares, POST /:id/shares, PATCH /:id/shares/:userId, DELETE /:id/shares/:userId. (3) Only the note owner (not write-access users) can manage shares. (4) Sharing a note with yourself (owner) is rejected. (5) HTTP status codes: 200 list/update, 201 create, 204 delete.

tasks.name: WebSocket Server Setup
tasks.description: Set up the WebSocket server attached to the same HTTP server as Express. This task is complete when: (1) `src/websocket/server.ts` creates a `ws.Server` instance attached to the HTTP server created in index.ts (not a standalone port). (2) On connection, the server reads the `token` query parameter from the WebSocket upgrade request URL. (3) The access token is verified using the same JWT_ACCESS_SECRET logic as the HTTP auth middleware. (4) If the token is missing or invalid, the WebSocket connection is terminated immediately with close code 4001. (5) On successful auth, the client's `WebSocket` instance is stored in an in-memory Map keyed by `userId` (e.g. `Map<string, WebSocket>`). If a user connects multiple times (e.g. multiple tabs), handle multiple connections per user — store as `Map<string, Set<WebSocket>>`. (6) On client disconnect, the client is removed from the in-memory map and any note rooms they were in. (7) `src/websocket/handlers.ts` exports a `handleMessage(ws, userId, rawMessage)` function that parses the JSON message, routes by `event` field, and calls the appropriate handler. (8) All incoming messages that are not valid JSON or missing the `event` field are silently ignored (no crash). (9) The WS server is initialized in `src/index.ts` after the HTTP server is created.

tasks.name: WebSocket Collaboration Events
tasks.description: Implement the full real-time note collaboration event handling over WebSockets. This task is complete when: (1) `note:join` event handler: resolves permission for the requesting user on the given noteId (at least 'read' required), adds the user to an in-memory note room (Map<noteId, Set<userId>>), fetches current note content from DB, sends `note:joined` back to the connecting client with { noteId, content, collaborators: [userId, ...] }, broadcasts `note:presence` to all other clients in the room. Sends `note:error` with code FORBIDDEN if user has no access, NOT_FOUND if note doesn't exist. (2) `note:leave` event handler: removes user from the room, broadcasts `note:presence` to remaining room members. (3) `note:update` event handler: resolves permission, requires 'write' access (sends note:error FORBIDDEN if only 'read'), saves new content to the Note in DB via Prisma, broadcasts `note:updated` (with noteId, content, updatedBy: userId) to all OTHER clients in the room (not back to sender). (4) On disconnect, the user is automatically removed from all rooms they were in, and `note:presence` is broadcast to affected rooms. (5) All broadcasts correctly target only clients in the specific note's room, not all connected clients. (6) Sending a message to a disconnected socket is handled gracefully (catch errors, remove dead socket from maps).

tasks.name: Auth Tests
tasks.description: Write a complete test suite covering the auth system. This task is complete when: (1) A test framework is configured — use Jest with ts-jest, plus supertest for HTTP integration tests. `npm test` runs all tests. (2) Test file at `backend/src/__tests__/auth.test.ts` (or similar). (3) Tests use an isolated in-memory or test SQLite database — not the dev database. The test DB is reset between test runs. (4) The following cases are all tested and passing: POST /api/auth/register — success 201 with user + tokens, 400 on missing email, 400 on missing password, 400 on password < 8 chars, 409 on duplicate email; POST /api/auth/login — success 200 with tokens, 401 on wrong password (same message as unknown email), 401 on unknown email, 400 on missing fields; POST /api/auth/refresh — success 200 with new tokens, 401 on invalid token string, 401 on token not found in DB, 401 on expired token, verifies old token is deleted (rotation); POST /api/auth/logout — success 204, idempotent (second logout does not error); Protected route — 401 when no Authorization header, 401 when token is malformed, 401 when token is expired, 200 when valid token. (5) No test leaks data to other tests. (6) All tests pass with `npm test`.

tasks.name: Users Tests
tasks.description: Write tests for the users endpoints. This task is complete when: (1) Tests at `backend/src/__tests__/users.test.ts`. (2) GET /api/users/me — returns correct user object (no password field), 401 if not authenticated. (3) GET /api/users/search?email=x — 200 with { id, email } for exact match, 404 for no match, 404 for partial match (e.g. searching 'alice' does not match 'alice@example.com'), 401 if not authenticated. (4) All tests pass.

tasks.name: Folders CRUD Tests
tasks.description: Write tests for all folder CRUD endpoints. This task is complete when: (1) Tests at `backend/src/__tests__/folders.test.ts`. (2) GET /api/folders — returns owned folders, returns shared folders with correct permission field, 401 if unauthenticated. (3) POST /api/folders — 201 with new folder (no parentId), 201 with nested folder when parent exists, 403 if creating inside a folder the user only has read access to, 404 if parentId does not exist, 400 if name missing. (4) PATCH /api/folders/:id — 200 on rename by owner, 200 on move to new parent by owner, 403 if not owner (even write-access users cannot rename folders), 404 if folder not found. (5) DELETE /api/folders/:id — 204 on success, recursively deletes child folders and notes (verify in DB after deletion), 403 if not owner, 404 if not found. (6) 401 on all routes when unauthenticated. (7) All tests pass.

tasks.name: Folder Sharing Tests
tasks.description: Write tests for folder sharing endpoints. This task is complete when: (1) Tests at `backend/src/__tests__/folder-shares.test.ts`. (2) GET /:id/shares — 200 with share list for owner, 403 for non-owner (even write-access), 401 unauthenticated. (3) POST /:id/shares — 201 on success, 404 if target email not found, 409 if already shared, 400 if permission invalid, 403 if requester is not owner, verify sharing with yourself (owner email) returns an error. (4) PATCH /:id/shares/:userId — 200 on success, 404 if share not found, 403 if not owner. (5) DELETE /:id/shares/:userId — 204 on success, 404 if share not found, 403 if not owner. (6) Verify that a user granted 'read' FolderShare can GET /api/folders and sees the shared folder with permission='read'. (7) Verify that a user granted 'write' FolderShare can create a note inside that folder. (8) All tests pass.

tasks.name: Notes CRUD Tests
tasks.description: Write tests for all note CRUD endpoints. This task is complete when: (1) Tests at `backend/src/__tests__/notes.test.ts`. (2) GET /api/notes — returns owned notes without `content` field, returns shared notes (via NoteShare and via FolderShare), correct `permission` field on each, optional folderId filter works, 401 unauthenticated. (3) POST /api/notes — 201 with full note including content, 201 with folderId, 400 on missing title, 403 if folderId exists but user has no write access, 404 if folderId not found. (4) GET /api/notes/:id — 200 with full content and permission field, 403 if user has no access, 404 if not found. (5) PATCH /api/notes/:id — 200 on update by owner, 200 on update by write-access user (NoteShare or FolderShare), 403 if user only has read access, 403 if user has no access, 404 if not found. (6) DELETE /api/notes/:id — 204 by owner, 403 if write-access user (not owner), 403 if read-access user, 404 if not found. (7) All tests pass.

tasks.name: Note Sharing Tests
tasks.description: Write tests for note sharing endpoints and permission resolution edge cases. This task is complete when: (1) Tests at `backend/src/__tests__/note-shares.test.ts`. (2) GET /:id/shares — 200 for owner, 403 for non-owner (even write-access), 401 unauthenticated. (3) POST /:id/shares — 201 on success, 404 target email not found, 409 already shared, 403 requester not owner, sharing with own email returns error. (4) PATCH /:id/shares/:userId — 200 success, 404 share not found, 403 not owner. (5) DELETE /:id/shares/:userId — 204 success, 404 not found, 403 not owner. (6) Permission resolution edge cases: user has direct NoteShare (write) AND FolderShare (read) — direct share takes precedence (write); user has direct NoteShare (read) AND FolderShare (write) — direct share takes precedence (read); user has only FolderShare — folder permission is used; note moved out of shared folder — user loses folder-based access to the note. (7) All tests pass.

tasks.name: WebSocket Tests
tasks.description: Write tests for the WebSocket real-time collaboration system. This task is complete when: (1) Tests at `backend/src/__tests__/websocket.test.ts`. Use the `ws` npm package as a client in tests. (2) Connection tests: connect with valid token succeeds, connect with invalid token is rejected (close code 4001), connect with no token is rejected. (3) note:join tests: join with valid noteId and read access receives note:joined with content and collaborators list; join with no access receives note:error with code FORBIDDEN; join with non-existent noteId receives note:error with code NOT_FOUND. (4) note:leave tests: after leaving, the user is no longer in the collaborators list broadcast by subsequent presence events. (5) note:update tests: user with write access sends note:update, content is saved in DB (verify via REST GET), other clients in the room receive note:updated event, sender does NOT receive note:updated (no echo), user with only read access sends note:update and receives note:error FORBIDDEN, DB is not modified. (6) Presence tests: when a second user joins, both clients receive note:presence with both userIds; when a user disconnects abruptly, remaining clients receive note:presence update. (7) Multi-client tests: at least two simultaneous WebSocket client connections are tested. (8) All tests pass.

tasks.name: End-to-End Smoke Test & Final Cleanup
tasks.description: Final validation that the entire backend works as an integrated system, and cleanup of any technical debt. This task is complete when: (1) `npm test` runs all test files and all tests pass with zero failures. (2) `npm run build` compiles with zero TypeScript errors. (3) There are no `any` types used anywhere in the codebase unless explicitly justified with a comment. (4) No `console.log` debug statements remain (only intentional `console.error` for unhandled errors in the error middleware and startup log are acceptable). (5) All environment variable accesses are validated at startup — if a required env var is missing, the server exits with a clear error message instead of crashing later. (6) The `GET /api/health` endpoint responds correctly. (7) A manual walkthrough of the full user flow works: register → login → create folder → create note → share note with second user → second user logs in and reads the note → second user edits note → WebSocket broadcasts the update → logout. (8) `backend/.env.example` is up to date with all required variables.
